subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #8 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Tabby and Tommy Cat
Inclusion
We are continuing to talk about consent and the importance of asking a friend
before
touching them or their belongings. We are also helping children practice both language and body signals they can use when a friend forgets to ask. For example:
“I am your friend, but please ask me before you touch my stuffy.”
(Hands up in a stop motion) “Please stop touching my hat. Please ask me before you touch it.”
We are also introducing the concept of Inclusion and 
how to invite friends to join your play.  We discuss how our body language also sends cues to our friends.
Gardening with Ms. Linda
We had a delivery of 1.5 cubic yards of topsoil and compost last Saturday. The children in each class took turns loading the wheelbarrow to deliver this rich soil to amend our garden plots. Many 
of the warm weather crops we grew last summer are heavy feeders including corn, tomatoes, peppers and cucumbers. To prepare for the next growing season we must add nutrients back into the soil. To keep our garden beds healthy we cleared them of dried stalks and leaves, whenever possible. However, we were careful to leave the seedpods intact for the wildlife that shares our ecosystem.
Diwali - Community Event
Earlier this week we celebrate Diwali with stories and making diyas.  This Saturday SOMA CrossWorks (a cross cultural organization) is hosting their annual family friendly Diwali Fest at Memorial Park (park side of the train station).
Click here for details
.
Friday - Pajama Pizza Cupcake 
Day!
This Friday we will celebrate Pizza Cupcake Day with our Pajamas - no costumes please.  A reminder that Tuesday, November 11th we will be closed in honor of Veterans Day.
Parent Teacher Conferences
Conferences are this week and the links are below.  If you can't make one of the times available please let me know and we will arrange a more convenient time.  Looking forward to great conversations about your wonderful children.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
PreK Orange Conference Sign Up
PreK Orange Zoom - Friday October 31st
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny and the children explored the differences between collages and sculptures, and then experimented with ways to make paper stand up on its own.
They also learned about
Charles McGee
, an American artist who created both collages and sculptures. The students noticed how he used contrasting colors to make certain elements stand out, and they practiced that in their own work.
Upcoming

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
